stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and performance tracking for Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission.
Musk claims OpenAI has shifted its focus from benefiting humanity to prioritizing profit.
The high-stakes legal battle could significantly impact the future direction of OpenAI and ChatGPT.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative-AI framework to modernize smart energy infrastructure.
Integrates advanced concepts like smart metering, GenAI, and quantum-inspired optimization.
Addresses critical utility functions: gas distribution, billing, carbon analytics, and infrastructure management.

stories.title: Researcher says Microsoft secretly built a backdoor into BitLocker
stories.author: techspot.com via oceanhaiyang
bullet_points: Allegation claims Microsoft secretly embedded a backdoor within the BitLocker encryption feature.
The story originates from a researcher's findings, suggesting a significant security vulnerability or intentional compromise.
This topic is of high relevance as it directly impacts enterprise and consumer data security infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes common ML frameworks like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline shown is for text-generation, indicating its generative capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own hardware for privacy and control.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tooling to the open-source community.

stories.title: Designing Datacenter Power Delivery Hierarchies for the AI Era
stories.author: Ricardo Bianchini
bullet_points: AI accelerator demand is driving datacenter rack power density towards 1MW per deployment by 2027.
This escalating power density presents a critical design challenge for current datacenter power delivery systems.
Future designs must ensure power delivery hierarchies remain efficient across long lifecycles and multiple hardware generations.

stories.title: Prospective multi-pathogen disease forecasting using autonomous LLM-guided tree search
stories.author: Zahra Shamsi
bullet_points: Introduces an autonomous system leveraging LLM-guided tree search for disease forecasting.
Addresses the scalability bottleneck in public health forecasting caused by manual model curation.
Aims to generate, evaluate, and optimize executable forecasting software in a prospective, real-time manner.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a novel protocol for self-evolving agent memory in LLM agents.
Allows agents to improve decision-making by generating reusable knowledge artifacts from failed trajectories without requiring weight updates (gradient updates).
Employs a population-based, staged approach wrapping a Reflexion-style inner loop using the same underlying LLM.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a method for LLM agents to build self-evolving memory without requiring weight updates (gradient descent).
FORGE uses a population-based protocol that wraps a Reflexion-style inner loop to convert failed agent trajectories into reusable knowledge artifacts.
The approach utilizes a dedicated reflection agent, operating on the same underlying LLM, to generate and refine prompt-injected natural-language memory for ReAct agents.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative AI framework specifically for smart energy infrastructure management.
Integrates advanced concepts like smart metering, generative AI, and quantum-inspired optimization.
Addresses critical utility functions including gas distribution, billing, carbon analytics, and infrastructure optimization.

stories.title: Universal Magnetic Structure Prediction from Atomic Coordinates with Near-Experimental Accuracy
stories.author: Mingda Li
bullet_points: Introduces Magnetic Structure Network (MSN), an E(3) equivariant graph neural network.
Aims to predict magnetic structure from atomic coordinates with near-experimental accuracy.
Addresses limitations of current methods in handling complex, noncollinear, and incommensurate magnetic orders.

stories.title: Artificial Aphasias in Lesioned Language Models
stories.author: Cory Shain
bullet_points: The research proposes an 'aphasia-inspired' technique to study the functional organization of Language Models (LMs).
The method involves 'lesioning' (zeroing out) specific model parameters and observing the resulting effects.
The goal is to draw parallels between clinical language impairments (aphasias) and the emergent structure of AI language models.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: Investigates the influence of AI-mediated communication on collective opinion formation.
Focuses on how LLMs integrated into platforms (like LinkedIn and X) shape human-to-human discourse.
Addresses a gap in research concerning AI's impact on group opinion, moving beyond individual human-AI interactions.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative AI framework to modernize energy infrastructure management.
Integrates advanced concepts like smart metering, generative AI, and quantum-inspired optimization.
Addresses critical utility functions including gas distribution, billing, and carbon analytics.

stories.title: Fully Open Meditron: An Auditable Pipeline for Clinical LLMs
stories.author: Mary-Anne Hartley
bullet_points: Introduces 'Fully Open Meditron,' a new pipeline designed for clinical LLMs.
Addresses the critical lack of transparency and auditability in current LLM-based Clinical Decision Support Systems (CDSS).
Distinguishes 'Fully Open' models from current 'open-weight' models by exposing the entire training stack, including data provenance and pipelines.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality within the Claude Code environment.
This integration enhances the capabilities of local LLM deployments (via Ollama) for complex coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It integrates various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local generation on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux versions is planned for the near future.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Elon Musk loses his case against Sam Altman
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: The high-profile lawsuit, Musk v. Altman, has concluded with a jury verdict.
The jury found that two of the claims were barred by the statute of limitations.
A third claim failed due to the dismissal of one of the underlying issues.

stories.title: Elon Musk took too long to sue OpenAI, jury unanimously agrees
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Elon Musk sued OpenAI, leading to a jury verdict.
The jury unanimously agreed with the ruling in the case.
Musk intends to appeal the decision, which a judge immediately affirmed.

stories.title: Bug bounty businesses bombarded with AI slop
stories.author: Jamie John, Financial Times
bullet_points: The proliferation of low-quality, AI-generated security vulnerabilities ('AI slop') is overwhelming bug bounty programs.
This influx of automated, often trivial, submissions is straining the capacity and reward structures of bug bounty businesses.
The story highlights a growing operational challenge in the cybersecurity industry due to generative AI misuse.

stories.title: The US space enterprise is desperately waiting for Starship—will it finally deliver?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: The US space enterprise's progress is heavily dependent on the success of Starship.
The development of Starship is characterized by extreme volatility, featuring both significant successes and major setbacks.
The article suggests a high level of anticipation and uncertainty surrounding Starship's ability to fulfill its promises.

stories.title: IVGT: Implicit Visual Geometry Transformer for Neural Scene Representation
stories.author: Jiwen Lu
bullet_points: Introduces IVGT, an Implicit Visual Geometry Transformer, to address the challenge of 3D reconstruction.
IVGT aims to implicitly model continuous and coherent 3D geometry from unposed, multi-view images.
It addresses limitations of existing models that rely on explicit geometry prediction (e.g., pixel-aligned pointmaps).

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a generative AI framework to automate the creation of natural-language utility bills.
The framework integrates carbon footprint tracking (CO2 per kWh) and grid load scheduling.
It combines a generative AI agent with a transformer-based forecaster for end-to-end resource optimization.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: The study investigates how AI-mediated communication influences collective opinion formation among humans.
It addresses a gap in research by focusing on AI's role in human-to-human interactions, rather than just human-AI interactions.
The context involves the increasing integration of LLMs into platforms like LinkedIn and X to polish and contextualize user content.

stories.title: Offline Semantic Guidance for Efficient Vision-Language-Action Policy Distillation
stories.author: Yishun Lu
bullet_points: Addresses the high computational cost of large Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies used in robotic manipulation.
Introduces VLA-AD, a distillation framework that uses a Vision-Language Model as an offline semantic supervisor.
Aims to transfer knowledge from large 'teacher' VLA models to lightweight 'student' policies for real-time control.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The study highlights a critical distinction between two methods for testing transformer layer redundancy: 'replacement' and 'interchange'.
These two tests, though both output-grounded, do not always yield the same results regarding layer equivalence.
The difference in testing protocols (the 'protocol gap') can significantly alter which layers are deemed safe for pruning in pretrained transformers.

stories.title: Evaluating Design Video Generation: Metrics for Compositional Fidelity
stories.author: Purvanshi Mehta
bullet_points: Addresses the lack of standardized evaluation metrics for generative video models used in design animation.
Highlights the unique constraints of design animation, such as prescribed motion, stable backgrounds, and preserved layout.
Proposes a fully automated evaluation framework structured across four key dimensions to assess compositional fidelity.

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a generative AI framework to address the industry need for carbon-aware utility billing.
The framework integrates a generative AI agent to draft natural-language billing statements from structured data.
It includes a transformer-based forecaster designed to manage load scheduling against grid stress and emissions constraints.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: The research investigates how AI-mediated communication influences collective opinion formation online.
It addresses a gap in prior research by focusing on human-to-human interactions mediated by AI, rather than just human-AI interactions.
The study examines the role of LLMs in platforms like LinkedIn and X in shaping group sentiment.

stories.title: Dynamics-Level Watermarking of Flow Matching Models with Random Codes
stories.author: Shuchan Wang
bullet_points: Introduces a novel dynamics-level watermarking technique for generative models, specifically flow matching models.
The watermark is embedded directly into the continuous dynamics (velocity field) rather than model weights or outputs.
The method uses random coding over a continuous channel, allowing for black-box detection queries.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical ambiguity in assessing transformer layer redundancy for model compression.
It distinguishes between 'replacement' (substitution) and 'interchange' (commutation) tests for layer equivalence.
The study shows that the choice of evaluation protocol (replacement vs. interchange) can drastically alter which layers are deemed safe to prune.

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes an end-to-end generative AI framework for utility billing.
The system drafts natural-language billing statements from structured data, incorporating carbon metrics.
It integrates a transformer-based forecaster to manage load against grid stress and emissions constraints.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical ambiguity in evaluating transformer layer redundancy for model compression.
It distinguishes between 'replacement' (substitutability) and 'interchange' (commutativity) tests for layer equivalence.
The study highlights that the choice of evaluation protocol can significantly alter which layers are deemed safe to prune in pretrained transformers.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a method for self-evolving agent memory in LLM agents without requiring weight updates.
Uses a population-based protocol and a Reflexion-style inner loop to convert failed trajectories into reusable knowledge artifacts.
Applies to hierarchical ReAct agents, allowing agents to learn from failures using prompt-injected natural-language memory.

stories.title: Artificial Aphasias in Lesioned Language Models
stories.author: Cory Shain
bullet_points: The research introduces an 'aphasia-inspired' technique to study the functional organization of Language Models (LMs).
The method involves 'lesioning' (zeroing out) specific model parameters to simulate language impairment.
The effects of these interventions are then measured against clinical aphasia symptom profiles to gain insights into LM structure.

stories.title: Argus: Evidence Assembly for Scalable Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Xinyu Wang
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Argus,' a method designed to improve deep research agents by addressing limitations in current parallel search and aggregation techniques.
Current state-of-the-art agents often duplicate evidence during parallel rollouts instead of finding complementary pieces, leading to diminishing returns.
Argus aims to efficiently assemble diverse, complementary pieces of evidence required for high-quality, deep research answers.

stories.title: Confirming Correct, Missing the Rest: LLM Tutoring Agents Struggle Where Feedback Matters Most
stories.author: Tiffany Barnes
bullet_points: The study evaluates LLM tutoring agents on their ability to provide nuanced feedback, specifically distinguishing between correct, valid-but-suboptimal, and incorrect student solutions.
It addresses a critical gap in current LLM applications: the lack of robust diagnostic precision when acting as conversational complements to Intelligent Tutoring Systems (ITS).
The research utilizes a large benchmark (10,836 pairs) in propositional logic, grounded by knowledge-graph-derived truth for rigorous testing.

stories.title: 🚀 Democratizing Frontier AI for Bharat: Gemma 4’s Edge Capabilities in Low-Resource Environments
stories.author: pravesh_sudha_3c2b0c2b5e0
bullet_points: The article focuses on leveraging Gemma 4's open-weight capabilities.
A key theme is democratizing Frontier AI specifically for low-resource environments in Bharat (India).
It is presented as a submission for a specific 'Gemma 4 Challenge'.

stories.title: I Ran AI Models Directly in the Browser and Measured What It Did to Core Web Vitals
stories.author: mspk97
bullet_points: The article investigates running AI models directly within the web browser.
It measures the performance impact of in-browser AI on Core Web Vitals.
The context is the growing trend of shipping AI features (e.g., sentiment analysis, speech recognition).

stories.title: I red-teamed Oracle APEX 26.1's new AI Agent feature in the 72 hours after it went GA. Claude refused 7 of my 10 attacks on its own.
stories.author: rkondoju
bullet_points: A security red-teaming exercise was conducted on Oracle APEX 26.1's new AI Agent feature.
The testing involved mapping 10 attack patterns against Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6.
The analysis revealed specific attack classes that succeeded despite Claude's initial refusal of several attempts.

stories.title: Shipping AI Agents Like A Pro
stories.author: wassimchegham
bullet_points: Focuses on production readiness for AI agents, moving beyond just model training.
Highlights critical engineering concerns like idempotency, validation, and tracing.
Addresses operational aspects such as budget management for deployed AI systems.

stories.title: How Gemma 4's Per-Layer Embeddings Actually Work — And Why E2B Punches Above 2B
stories.author: shreya111111
bullet_points: The article delves into the technical implementation of Per-Layer Embeddings within the Gemma 4 model.
It compares the performance of a specific model variant (E2B) against a baseline (2B), suggesting a performance advantage.
The content is highly technical, targeting an audience interested in deep learning architecture and LLM internals.
